paper_title: Semi-chronic toxicity study of the extracts from different parts of burmese-grape Baccaurea ramiflora fruits of Ha Chau variety using mouse model

entities.description: The study of adverse effects resulting from repeated exposure to a substance over a portion of an organism's lifespan.
Burmese-grape, the plant species whose fruit parts were extracted for study.
The ability of compounds to prevent oxidative stress related diseases.
The study of changes in tissues caused by disease or toxicity.
Process of using 96% ethanol to obtain crude extracts from seeds and peels.
Equipment used for drying plant parts at 50±2 oC.
Equipment used to evaporate filtrates at 58±2 oC to dryness.
The animal model used for the semi-chronic toxicity assay.
Directly inserting extracts into the stomach/esophagus of mice via a needle.
Statistical software used for data analysis.
The concentration of extract administered to mice daily.
The percentage increase in body weight observed after 45 days.
Measured glucose levels in mice after 45 days of treatment.

entities.id: Ratio of kidney weights to body weights (2.53±0.24%)
entities.description: Significant ratio observed in the peel extract group after 90 days.
The scientific study of the adverse effects of chemical substances on living organisms.
Researcher from Can Tho University and Can Tho University of Technology.
Researcher from the Institute of Food and Biotechnology, Can Tho University.

entities.id: College of Engineering Technology, Can Tho University

relations.target: Ratio of kidney weights to body weights (2.53±0.24%)
